commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fd8ed4386f7232d3f35bc146c4d532ce24458369)
commands.review_summary:
The PR moves Vitest-backed Plugin SDK test helpers into a private `@openclaw/plugin-test-kit` workspace package, replaces deprecated public helper subpaths with import-safe shims, adds a release guard for Vitest imports, and updates plugin testing docs.

PR surface: Source +807, Tests +64, Docs -3, Config +91, Other +45. Total +1004 across 36 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main's public SDK helper subpaths re-export files that import `vitest`, and the PR body also describes the Kova clean-package failure. I did not rerun a clean installed-package lane in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer compatibility judgment for shipped Plugin SDK helper semantics plus an author docs fix; this is not a safe autonomous cleanup or close item.

target.title: fix: isolate plugin test helpers from sdk exports
target.body:
## Kova repro this fixes

Kova hit the package-boundary failure this PR is meant to fix: a clean consumer of `openclaw@beta` could import a public Plugin SDK test-contract subpath and crash with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND: Cannot find package 'vitest'`. The public `openclaw` package was exposing Vitest-backed test helper entrypoints, even though `vitest` is a dev-only repo dependency.

This PR keeps those deprecated public subpaths importable during the compatibility window, but replaces their runtime implementations with tiny import-safe shims that do not load Vitest. OpenClaw repo tests still get the real helpers through the private workspace package `@openclaw/plugin-test-kit` via Vitest and tsconfig aliases.

## Bundle / download size

Measured against clean `origin/main` at `c8d20aeb48` after `pnpm build` and `npm pack --dry-run --ignore-scripts --json`.

| Metric | Before | After | Delta |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| npm tarball/download size | 17,657,096 bytes | 17,572,216 bytes | -84,880 bytes |
| unpacked package size | 78,337,711 bytes | 77,978,154 bytes | -359,557 bytes |
| packed entries | 12,569 | 12,419 | -150 |
| deprecated Plugin SDK JS entrypoints | 178,882 bytes | 34,782 bytes | -144,100 bytes |
| all `dist/plugin-sdk` files | 7,233,082 bytes | 7,014,991 bytes | -218,091 bytes |

## Summary

- Add `packages/plugin-test-kit` as the private repo-only home for the old Vitest-backed Plugin SDK test helpers.
- Point repo Vitest and tsconfig resolution for deprecated SDK test-helper subpaths at `@openclaw/plugin-test-kit`, avoiding a mass import rewrite.
- Replace deprecated public Plugin SDK test-helper runtime exports with import-safe compatibility shims, while preserving type-only compatibility exports where existing consumers may use `import type`.
- Add a release-check guard that scans built `dist/plugin-sdk/*.js` and fails if any public Plugin SDK package entrypoint statically imports `vitest`.
- Update plugin testing docs to stop recommending the deprecated public test-helper subpaths for external plugin tests.

## Re-export compatibility note

The re-export shape is a compatibility bridge, not the new architecture pattern. Runtime value exports from the deprecated public subpaths now all point at a small proxy that throws a clear migration error if called from the published package. Type-only exports are kept or inlined so existing TypeScript consumers using `import type` do not break during the documented compatibility window. New test helper implementation belongs in `@openclaw/plugin-test-kit`, not in public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` package exports.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/release-check.test.ts test/vitest-scoped-config.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `npm pack --dry-run --ignore-scripts --json > /tmp/openclaw-pack-after.json`
- Static built dist scan with `collectPluginSdkVitestImportErrors`: `plugin-sdk Vitest import guard: passed`
- Built package export import smoke: `imported 316 plugin SDK package exports`
- `git diff --check`
- Autoreview first pass found the type-only export compatibility gap; fixed by preserving/inlining public type exports. A second autoreview rerun was interrupted because it was hanging, so final proof is the focused tests/build/package/import checks above.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: public Plugin SDK test-helper package exports could require dev-only `vitest` from a clean installed `openclaw@beta` package, as seen in the Kova clean package import failure.
Real environment tested: local source checkout after patch with built package artifacts from `pnpm build`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest contract tests, `pnpm build`, npm dry-run pack size measurement, static `dist/plugin-sdk` Vitest import scan, and import smoke for every `openclaw/plugin-sdk*` package export.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed; build passed; static dist scan reported no Vitest imports in built Plugin SDK package entrypoints; package export smoke imported 316 Plugin SDK exports.
Observed result after fix: deprecated public SDK test-helper subpaths remain importable without loading Vitest, while repo tests still resolve the real Vitest-backed helpers through the private `@openclaw/plugin-test-kit` workspace boundary.
What was not tested: the full Kova lane was not rerun in this session; the local package-boundary proof covers the reported missing-`vitest` failure mode directly.
target.author: romneyda
